name: leios_votes_before_offer
description: The server delivers three votes for eb_hash=abab.. BEFORE the block_offer for that EB is sent. In a real network, vote messages propagate independently of EB body announcements and may arrive first due to network timing. A conforming node must not crash or enter an error state when votes arrive for an as-yet-unannounced EB; it may buffer them speculatively or discard them, but must continue to process subsequent notifications normally. The block_offer is sent after the votes so the node sees the canonical ordering used in the fetch decision (offer triggers the Ftch-Action precondition). This scenario is the ordering complement of leios_vote_for_unannounced_eb: here the offer does eventually arrive; there it never does.
